use_case_description: The system is a real-time exercise management display tool used by authorized personnel — such as exercise coordinators and operations staff — to monitor and track Events and Activities during homeland security exercises. It presents a dual-region interface combining a graphical timeline view with a structured data table, pulling live data from the Sync Matrix and Exercise Management Tool applications and refreshing automatically to keep information current. Users can filter, customize, and interact with the display to focus on relevant venues and key events, with color-coded indicators highlighting how closely ongoing activities align with their scheduled times. The system also supports historical reporting and direct links back to source applications for data modifications, ensuring coordinators can manage exercise execution from a single, integrated view.

use_case_description: This system is a mobile real estate application designed for use by licensed realtors on smart devices, enabling them to manage property listings and client relationships while working in the field. Realtors can search MLS listings, view property images and maps, get driving directions, and generate Comparative Market Analysis (CMA) reports to support property valuations and client presentations. The system also handles scheduling and communication by allowing realtors to call clients, send appointment requests, receive responses, and stay synchronized with their office systems for up-to-date contact and calendar information. Designed to be intuitive and self-installing, the application requires no formal training and operates seamlessly across both connected and offline environments to ensure continuous productivity. Access is restricted to authenticated and authorized realtors, and the platform is built to scale to support thousands of simultaneous users across multiple device platforms.

use_case_description: This system is a nursing program management and scheduling platform used primarily by Program Administrators and Nursing Staff Members at DePaul University's Nursing Department. It enables staff to organize and maintain cohorts of nursing students, define Programs of Study, and manage the scheduling of both classroom and clinical courses across multiple quarters. The system tracks individual student progress, manages clinical site assignments and lab sections, and generates printable reports for cohorts, students, clinical sites, and lab sections to support administrative planning. It automatically notifies students, instructors, and other relevant parties via email when changes occur to schedules, clinical sections, or cohort arrangements. Designed to integrate with existing university systems such as CampusConnect and faculty and student servers, the platform enforces role-based access controls to protect sensitive student and clinical site information while providing a reliable, easy-to-use environment for day-to-day nursing program administration.

use_case_description: The Disputes application is a web-based case management system used by internal cardmember services representatives, external merchant services representatives, and system administrators to initiate and manage credit card dispute cases. The system allows authorized users to search for existing disputes, create ticket retrieval requests or chargeback notifications against disputed transactions, and perform follow-up actions on open cases throughout their lifecycle. It enforces role-based access controls to ensure users can only view or modify dispute cases within their authorized cardholder base, and it maintains a complete audit trail of all user and system-generated activity on every case. The application integrates with several back-end systems — including cardmember and merchant databases, a billing system, and a letter printing utility — to retrieve account information, process financial adjustments, and generate correspondence to merchants. All business rules and processes within the system comply with applicable financial regulations, including Regulation E, Regulation Z, and Merchant Operating Regulations.

use_case_description: This software system supports insurance claims processing by enabling collision estimators and adjusters to efficiently manage vehicle repair estimates with an emphasis on recycled parts usage. Collision estimators use the system to search for available recycled parts from preferred suppliers and apply them to collision estimates, while adjusters assign preferred repair facilities, review completed estimates, and request audit reports that evaluate how effectively recycled parts were incorporated. Supervisory adjusters can also rate repair facilities based on audit outcomes, helping to guide future facility assignments. The system integrates with the ChoiceParts external data feed to source recycled parts inventory and enforces role-based access to ensure that each user type interacts only with the functions appropriate to their responsibilities. Designed for use in both office and repair shop environments, the system serves multiple insurance companies while keeping their claims data strictly isolated from one another.

use_case_description: This web-based conference room and meeting management system enables employees, meeting organizers, and managers within a company to schedule meetings, reserve conference rooms, and manage associated equipment across one or more office buildings. Employees can search for available rooms, send and respond to meeting invitations, receive reminders and notifications, and view building maps showing room locations, while the system automatically updates room schedules and tracks equipment reservations and transport requests. Meeting organizers can manage agendas, invite attendees, and receive confirmations, while managers have elevated access to run reservation reports and view meeting schedules across multiple views. The system is a Java-based, browser-accessible enterprise application that integrates with standard database management systems and email servers, supporting large numbers of concurrent users across potentially global, multilingual organizations. It is designed to be highly configurable, brandable to the customer's organization, and maintainable over a multi-year lifecycle with minimal administrative overhead.

use_case_description: The system is a restaurant management platform used by business staff — including inventory managers, formula administrators, and point-of-sale operators — to manage product formulas, menus, and inventory adjustments. It automatically calculates and generates Inventory Quantity Adjustment documents based on daily sales data and product formulas, accounting for substitutionary ingredients and current inventory levels. Staff can define and maintain product formulas with support for semi-products and ingredient substitutions, as well as build and organize hierarchical product menus that integrate with POS terminals. The system enforces role-based access control, maintains audit-compliant accounting standards, and supports multiple concurrent users within a secure, Windows-based environment running on existing hardware.

use_case_description: IzognMovies is a web-based streaming platform that allows customers to discover, purchase, and watch African movies on demand through their web browser. Customers can register for either a pay-as-you-go or unlimited subscription plan, browse and search the movie catalog, pay via credit card or pre-paid card, and stream purchased films for a two-day viewing window. The platform also supports a community experience through customer movie reviews and personalized account management. Izogn administrators manage the movie catalog, customer accounts, categories, pre-paid card activation, and content moderation, while the Izogn Manager monitors sales and usage analytics. The system is designed to be visually engaging and culturally resonant for African audiences — particularly Nigerians — while remaining accessible and welcoming to a broad global user base.

use_case_description: The Lead Washing System is an automated web service that receives, validates, scores, and routes incoming prospective student leads submitted by external vendors. Upon receipt, the system evaluates each lead against configurable corporate and school-defined parameters, assigning a composite score based on contact and academic criteria to determine the appropriate disposition. High-scoring leads are automatically forwarded to the enterprise system and assigned to an admissions representative, mid-range leads are queued for manual review by Enrollment Coordinators, and low-scoring or invalid leads are rejected and returned to the originating vendor with a reason for rejection. Vendors whose leads are rejected may submit credit validation records to the enterprise billing system, while duplicate leads are flagged and stored for reference. Administrative users maintain scoring parameters and access defect reports through a secured interface, with the entire process designed to operate within strict processing time thresholds to support downstream auto-representative assignment workflows.

use_case_description: This system is an online, multiplayer Battleship game that allows two players to compete against each other in real time over the internet. Players register themselves in a shared lobby, select an available opponent, and engage in a turn-based naval combat game in which each player secretly positions a fleet of ships on a personal defensive grid and takes shots at their opponent's hidden fleet. The interface is styled with a nautical theme — presenting grids through a periscope-style view, rendering ships as realistic images, and accompanying gameplay events such as hits, misses, and sinkings with appropriate sound effects. The system manages turn progression automatically, tracking hits and misses on both players' grids until one player's entire fleet is sunk or a player chooses to forfeit. Designed for a broad international audience, the system supports English, Spanish, and French, and is built to be continuously available, highly concurrent, and intuitive enough for new users to play without any prior training.

use_case_description: The system is a web-based scheduling management tool used by callers and supervisors at the DePaul TeleServices Center. Supervisors can enter and manage caller schedules, view the schedules of all callers, and advertise open time slots, while callers can access their own scheduling information through the system. The application is designed to be intuitive and visually appealing, allowing both user groups to learn and complete tasks quickly without extensive training. It operates through standard web browsers and must remain highly available throughout the day to support the center's operational hours.

use_case_description: The system is an enterprise messaging and integration platform developed for the City of Chicago that facilitates real-time, asynchronous message exchange between disparate systems — most notably between the Customer Service Request (CSR) system and the Enterprise Asset Management (EAM/Datastream) system — without requiring direct interfaces between them. System Administrators and Database Administrators use the platform to monitor message traffic, track and resolve transaction errors, and analyze system performance through dashboards and ad-hoc reporting with key performance indicators. The system is designed to handle high transaction volumes reliably, processing messages within strict time thresholds while maintaining near-continuous availability during regular business operations. It supports dynamic scaling and transparent resource additions to accommodate growth and changing workloads without service interruption. Security is enforced through authentication, authorization, encryption, and integrity checking, and all messages are retained for audit and transaction tracking purposes.

use_case_description: The Worldwide Community System (WCS) is an internal web-based reporting tool used by authorized IBM Corporate Community Relations (CCR) employees to access, analyze, and export community grants data. The system allows CCR users and management to generate and view summarized reports through a simple, point-and-click interface that adheres to IBM and CCR branding and usability standards. Users can export reports in common spreadsheet formats such as Microsoft Excel and Lotus 1-2-3 with minimal effort, and the system refreshes its underlying data on a weekly basis to ensure information remains current. Access is secured through IBM intranet authentication, and the system is designed to be intuitive enough that users can navigate it independently after a single use.

use_case_description: This web-based application serves as a fantasy football league management platform used by league commissioners and team owners. Team owners can manage their rosters by adding, dropping, and trading NFL players, while viewing up-to-date player statistics and fantasy points to inform their decisions. The commissioner holds elevated privileges to configure and adjust league-wide settings, ensuring proper governance of the league. A message board on the league page keeps participants engaged by surfacing recent community posts, and the system's intuitive navigation allows users to quickly move between league and team pages. Built for high availability and rapid response times, the platform is designed to reliably support all user transactions throughout the NFL season.

use_case_description: The Revenue and Expense Forecasting System (RFS) is an intranet-based budgeting application used by finance staff and managers across PCG's organizational hierarchy, including branch, complex, district, and regional levels. The system enables authorized users to complete their annual budget submissions by working with revenue and expense data loaded directly from the Business Data Warehouse (BDW), with calculations driven by formulae that are updated each year prior to the budgeting cycle. Access to the system is controlled through integration with a central User Administration System (UAS), ensuring that each user can only view and interact with the functionality appropriate to their role within the PCG hierarchy. The system is designed to be highly available and responsive, particularly during the critical budgeting period, and must conform to ENET Securities' intranet standards for look, feel, and browser compatibility.

ground_truth_requirements.label: NFR

Test files (7) shall be loaded up to 5 times throughout the year from the BDW.
